summary: Matched src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ShieldBreakDown.c to 100%, replacing inline MWERKS assembly for ftCo_80098E3C and the ShieldBreakDown anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks with C. The PR also split related ShieldBreakStand code out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c/.h translation unit, added it to obj_files.mk, and promoted ftCo_80099010 to a global asm symbol/prototype so remaining stand-side code could call it cleanly.
what_changed: Removed the inline assembly implementation of ftCo_80098E3C from ftCo_ShieldBreakDown.c and rewrote it in C.
Implemented ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Anim in C as an ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining guard that calls ftCo_80098F3C when the animation completes.
Implemented ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_IASA as an empty callback, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Phys as a wrapper around ft_80084F3C, and ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Coll as a wrapper around ft_80083F88.
Added declarations for ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Anim, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_IASA, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Phys, and ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Coll to ftCo_ShieldBreakDown.h.
Created ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c and ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.h, moving the ShieldBreakStand functions and constants ftCo_804D8668/ftCo_804D866C out of ft_08A4.s into their own source/header area.
Changed the remaining assembly label .L_80099010 in asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into global symbol ftCo_80099010 and added an UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototype for it in ft_08A4.h.

The PR report states src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ShieldBreakDown.c reached 100.00% for the file and for ftCo_80098E3C, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Anim, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Coll, ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Phys, and ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_IASA.
smart_moves: Used existing enum and flag names in the C rewrite: GA_Air, FtMotionId, ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownU, ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownD, Ft_MF_KeepColAnimHitStatus, Ft_MF_SkipModel, Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim, and Ft_MF_SkipColAnim. This made the assembly constants 0xcf/0xd0 and 0x1094 readable without changing behavior.

Preserved the motion-state decision logic from assembly: call ftCommon_8007D7FC when the fighter is airborne, choose the down-up or down-down shield-break motion from ftCo_80097570, call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with start frame NULL, rate 0, anim speed 1, and arg 0, then call ftCo_800978D4.
Kept tiny callback wrappers simple rather than overfitting them: IASA is an empty function, Phys forwards to ft_80084F3C, and Coll forwards to ft_80083F88.

Split ShieldBreakStand into its own file while leaving still-unmatched stand functions as inline assembly, allowing the ShieldBreakDown file to become fully matched without requiring all neighboring code to be decompiled at once.
Promoted the local assembly label .L_80099010 to ftCo_80099010 so the new ShieldBreakStand assembly wrapper could reference a named symbol across translation-unit boundaries.
decomp_lessons: A file can often be fully matched by isolating unrelated or not-yet-matched neighboring functions into a separate translation unit rather than keeping a broad asm chunk together.

When moving functions out of an asm file, remember to move associated sdata2 constants as well; this PR moved ftCo_804D8668 and ftCo_804D866C into ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c while the down-side constants remained in ftCo_ShieldBreakDown.c.

For action-state callbacks, many Phys and Coll functions are direct calls to common helpers. Matching can be achieved by writing minimal wrapper C functions with the same argument type and no extra local variables.

Use known motion-state IDs and motion-flag macros to replace raw immediates. Here, raw motion IDs 0xcf/0xd0 were represented as ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownU/ftCo_MS_ShieldBreakDownD, and raw flag 0x1094 was expressed as a bitwise OR of Ft_MF_* flags.
If a local asm label must be called from a newly split source file, rename it into a global placeholder-style symbol and declare it in an existing header until its function is fully decompiled.
naming_conventions: Common fighter shield-break files use ftCo_ShieldBreakDown and ftCo_ShieldBreakStand file/function prefixes.
Matched action callbacks follow the suffix pattern _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Address-based unknown helpers retained ftCo_80098E3C, ftCo_80098F3C, and ftCo_80099010 names when descriptive names were not established.
Headers use include guards matching the project path style, e.g. MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCOMMON_FTCO_SHIELDBREAKSTAND_H.
Function declarations in headers include address comments such as /* 098F3C */ and /* 098A44 */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced inline MWERKS asm for ftCo_80098E3C with structured C using a temporary FtMotionId variable inside a scoped block.
Matched ftCo_ShieldBreakDown_Anim with a negated ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining condition and a call to ftCo_80098F3C, mirroring the assembly branch-on-nonzero frame check.
Preserved the exact Fighter_ChangeMotionState argument shape: gobj, chosen motion state, ORed motion flags, NULL, 0, 1, 0.
Used ftCo_Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data and fp->ground_or_air == GA_Air to match the assembly load from gobj->user_data and compare against airborne state.
Kept remaining ShieldBreakStand functions under #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm in the new C file instead of forcing incomplete C decompilation.
Converted .L_80099010 in ft_08A4.s to a global ftCo_80099010 symbol to preserve linkability after splitting code out of the asm file.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompiled ftCo_ShieldBreakStand.c or replaced the remaining MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm for ftCo_80098F3C, ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Anim, ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Phys, and ftCo_ShieldBreakStand_Coll.
Search for later renames or typed signatures for ftCo_80099010 after it was introduced as a global UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototype.
Look for related shield-break PRs covering ftCo_ShieldBreakFly, ftCo_ShieldBreakFall, or ftCo_DownBound for reusable callback and motion-state matching patterns.
